title: 10th Pass Jobs 2026 – Driver (Staff Car)
shortInfo: Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL) Recruitment 2026 for 2 posts of Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis. Candidates with 10th pass Can Apply Online. The online application opens on 25-05-2026, and closes on 09-06-2026. The candidate shall apply online through CSL official website.

basic_details.short_information: Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL) Recruitment 2026 for 2 posts of Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis. Candidates with 10th pass Can Apply Online. The online application opens on 25-05-2026, and closes on 09-06-2026. The candidate shall apply online through CSL official website.

age_limit.details: Candidates should download and read the CSL Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below.

qualification.education_qualification: Pass in X Std and possess Valid Light Motor Vehicle Driving License
qualification.details: Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis: Pass in X Std and possess Valid Light Motor Vehicle Driving License. Minimum 10 years experience as a Driver, out of which at least 1 year experience as Staff Car Driver for Senior Officers such as Chairman & Managing Director (CMD), Director, Chief Vigilance Officer (CVO), Chief Executive Officer (CEO), or Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs)/State Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) or officials of equivalent rank in the Central/State Government.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 09-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CSL to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for the CSL Driver (Staff Car) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the CSL Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis posts is 09 June 2026.

faq.question: How many vacancies are available for the Driver post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 2 vacancies for the Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis position at Cochin Shipyard Limited.

faq.question: What is the required qualification for the Driver post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed X Std and possess a Valid Light Motor Vehicle Driving License, along with specific driving experience as detailed in the notification.

faq.question: What is the age limit for the Driver (Staff Car) post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Driver (Staff Car) on contract basis post is 55 years.

faq.question: What is the salary offered for this position?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 27,000/- per month.

faq.question: What is the application fee for General/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: The application fee for General and OBC category candidates is ₹ 200/-.

faq.question: Is there any application fee for SC/ST candidates?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for SC/ST candidates.

faq.question: What is the selection process for the Driver (Staff Car) recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process will primarily involve a Practical Test carrying 100 marks.

faq.question: How can I apply for the CSL Driver (Staff Car) recruitment?
faq.answer: Interested candidates can apply online through the official CSL website on or before the last date of application.

faq.question: Where can I find the official notification and apply link?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF and the online application link are provided in the "Important Links" section of this article.
